steps.title: Use rate times quantity
steps.content: The rate is $2$ dollars per pound. For $c$ pounds, multiply the rate by the number of pounds: $$2\cdot c=2c.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is correct because it multiplies dollars per pound by pounds. Choice B adds instead of multiplying. Choice C divides $2$ by the number of pounds. Choice D treats $c$ as the total cost for $2$ pounds instead of the number of pounds.

steps.title: Confirm with a quick number
steps.content: For example, if $c=3$, the cost should be $3\cdot \$2=\$6$. Choice A gives $2c=6$, so A is correct.
